title: Model Library Install button reverts mid-download (first install after reload, remote pod)
description:
On a remote/ephemeral Pod, pressing Install in the Model Library detail drawer sometimes flashed to Cancel then reverted to Install while the download was actually running (tile bar kept climbing). Intermittent: only the FIRST install after app open/reload. Finished installs always ended correctly (Uninstall), so the pipeline was fine — only the mid-flight footer/tile state was wrong.

ROOT (proven by console trace): downloadService.start() calls _ensureSSE(), which opens the SSE stream for the first time this session AND creates the client 'downloading' job in the same tick. The SSE 'open' handler then fetches /comfy/downloads/status and OVERWROTE state.downloadJobs with the backend list. On a fresh install that list held only OLD complete jobs (the just-started install not yet registered backend-side), so the overwrite WIPED the live 'downloading' job → _modelState saw no job → footer/tile reverted to Install. Once SSE was already open (2nd+ install), _ensureSSE no-ops and the race never fired — hence 'sometimes'.

FIX (js/services/downloadService.js SSE 'open'): MERGE instead of clobber — keep any ACTIVE client job (downloading/queued/paused/installing) whose modelId the backend list doesn't yet include; backend copy wins for shared ids. Preserves the reconnect-recovery purpose without dropping an in-flight install.

DEFENSIVE (js/components/.../MpiModelManager.js): also hardened the footer/tile state machine against the separate fast started->complete ephemeral window — a lingering terminal 'complete' job now counts as 'busy' (holds Cancel/progress, never flashes Install), and anyInstalled is checked BEFORE busy so Uninstall wins the instant re-sync flips installed. No new label (the pre-existing 'Verifying...' sweep is the only end-phase text).

Guard: tests/model-footer-settling.test.cjs (9 cases: footer branch order + SSE-open merge).
